title: Pristiglottis
intro: Pristiglottis is a genus of orchids commonly known as "glossy lip orchids" or "glossy lip Pristiglottis," characterized by their distinctive, glossy lip-like structure. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of digestive issues and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Pristiglottis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are thought to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Pristiglottis is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or dried powder, and is used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and digestive problems.
taxonomy: Pristiglottis is a genus of flowering plants in the family Polypodiaceae, commonly known as the polypody ferns. These ferns are native to tropical Asia. The genus Pristiglottis was first described by the Japanese botanist Makoto Nishida in 1937. Pristiglottis species are epiphytic or terrestrial ferns, which grow on other plants or on the ground. They are often found in humid, shaded environments in tropical regions.
